title: The Salt-Pork Tin
description: Pedrick produces a small tin of salt-pork fat from his coat pocket—enough to grease the oarlocks of three bateaux. He explains the trade-off in flat, factual terms: greased locks reduce acoustic signature to near-zero near the British warships but require a feathering stroke that cuts speed by roughly a third. Un-greased locks are faster but audible at fifty yards on a calm stretch. The choice determines whether the mid-channel lane runs fast and loud or slow and silent for the next two hours.
